questions.question_text:
In an apartment complex, the monthly electricity consumption (in units) for five flats is 320, 400, 360, 340, and 380. If the residents want to know the average monthly consumption (in units) per flat by using the direct method, what value will they obtain

Ans

X A. 350
‚úì B. 360
X C. 355
X D. 365

Q.2

Seven years ago Ramu was five times as old as his son. The present age difference between Ramu and his son is 28 years. Find the sum of their present ages.

Ans

X A. 28 years
X B. 73 years
‚úì C. 56 years
X D. 30 years

Q.3

The fourth proportional to three positive numbers is 120. If the first number is twice the second, and the third number is five times the first, then find the sum of all the three numbers.

Ans

‚úì A. 312
X B. 336
X C. 288
X D. 300

Q.4

Two trains of lengths 250 m and 300 m run in opposite directions at 72 km/hr and 108 km/hr. In how many seconds will they completely cross each other?

Ans

‚úì A. 11 seconds
X B. 12 seconds
X C. 15 seconds
X D. 14 seconds

Q.5

The sum of two positive numbers is 51 and their product is 230. The positive difference between them is:

Ans

X A. 52
‚úì B. 41
X C. 61
X D. 32

Q.6

Four prime numbers are listed in order. If the product of the first three is 105 and the product of the last three is 385, what is the largest prime number?

Ans

X A. 7
X B. 13
‚úì C. 11
X D. 17

Q.7

A recipe requires sugar and flour in the ratio 7:11. If you have 154 grams of sugar and want to maintain the same proportion, how much flour should you use?

Ans

X A. 264 grams
X B. 198 grams
X C. 220 grams
‚úì D. 242 grams

Q.8

The volume of a cylinder having height 27 cm and radius 7 cm is:
$(\pi = \frac{22}{7})$

Ans

X A. $4068 \text{ cm}^3$
‚úì B. $4158 \text{ cm}^3$
X C. $4146 \text{ cm}^3$
X D. $4187 \text{ cm}^3$

Q.9

Pipes A and B fill a tank in 20 and 30 minutes, respectively. If both are opened together, after how many minutes will the tank be $\frac{2}{3}$ full?

Ans

‚úì A. 8 minutes
X B. 12 minutes
X C. 10 minutes
X D. 16 minutes

Q.10

Simplify:
$$\frac{(3^5 \times 6^4 \times 4^3)}{(3^2 \times 6^2 \times 4^1)}$$

Ans

X A. 15559
‚úì B. 15552
X C. 15558
X D. 15554

Q.12

If the ratio of two numbers is 8 : 3, and their HCF is 8, then their LCM is:

Ans

X A. 200
‚úì B. 192
X C. 186
X D. 229

Q.13

Let p and q be distinct positive prime numbers such that $p > q$ and $p^2 - q^2 = 45$. What is the value of p + q?

Ans

‚úì A. 9
X B. 12
X C. 13
X D. 10

Q.14

A rectangular garden has a length of 16 meters and a width of x meters. If the width is the mean proportional between 4 meters and 64 meters, what is the value of x?

Ans

X A. 24
‚úì B. 16
X C. 12
X D. 8

Q.15

An investment decreases by 30% in the first year, increases by 30% in the second year, and then increases by 20% in the third year. What is the overall percentage change from the initial value?

Ans

X A. 9.2% decrease
X B. 10.2% decrease
X C. 10.2% increase
‚úì D. 9.2% increase

Q.16

There are 400 multiple choice questions in a test. 4 marks are allotted for a correct answer and 1 mark is deducted for a wrong answer or unattempted question from total score of correct answers. If a candidate has scored 1280 marks in the test, how many questions did the candidate answer correctly?

Ans

X A. 322
X B. 346
‚úì C. 336
X D. 315

Q.17

A merchant buys tea at ‚Çπ100 per kg and sells it using a 800 g weight as 1 kg, after marking the price up by 25% and giving a discount of 20%. What is his profit per kg?

Ans

X A. ‚Çπ10
X B. ‚Çπ15
X C. ‚Çπ20
‚úì D. ‚Çπ25

Q.18

A shopkeeper offers a fixed discount of 15% on rice and 100 gm rice free with every purchase of 1.9 kg rice. Find the overall discount percent offered by him on rice.

Ans

‚úì A. 19.25%
X B. 19.50%
X C. 19.10%
X D. 20.00%

Q.19

Find the value of m which satisfies
$$\left(\frac{23}{8}\right)^{12} \times \left(\frac{8}{23}\right)^{15} \times \left(\frac{23}{8}\right)^9 = \left(\frac{8}{23}\right)^{8m + 8}$$

Ans

X A. $-\frac{9}{8}$
X B. $-\frac{24}{8}$
‚úì C. $-\frac{7}{4}$
X D. $-\frac{10}{8}$
